Back to Yoga

Yoga pose

Tadasana

Mountain pose builds posture awareness, steady breathing, and full-body alignment.

How to do Tadasana

Safety, comfort, and awareness are the most important things to consider. Focus on one to three alignment points at a time, then add more as the pose becomes familiar.

Steps

  1. Stand with your big toes touching and your heels slightly apart.
  2. Distribute pressure evenly between your big toes, little toes, and heels.
  3. Lift your arches and press into the outer edges of your feet.
  4. Keep your knees slightly bent and lift your kneecaps gently.
  5. Bring your pelvis into a neutral position and activate your core.
  6. Elongate your spine, broaden your chest, and relax your arms by your sides.
  7. Take slow, deep breaths and hold for up to one minute.

Benefits

  • Improves flexibility and posture.
  • Builds strength and body awareness.
  • Supports healthy breathing patterns.
  • Helps with stress relief and balance.
Back to Yoga
